one_time_description: Prior period restatement: tangible capital assets understated due to asset not previously capitalized, restating 2022 TCA by $7,625,611
economic_notes: GBEs include real estate development and natural resources segments. Key entities include MST Development Corporation, multiple Eagle limited partnerships, Takaya Developments, SPAL General Constructors, TWN Renewable Energy, and Tsleil-Waututh Land Limited Partnership. Olympic Legacy Trust invested through RBC Dominion Securities and TD Waterhouse totalling $29,652,366.
notes: Prior period restatement for FY2022 due to previously uncapitalized tangible capital asset ($7,625,611). GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis with 30 entities. GBEs reported a net loss of $3,111,496 in 2023 vs income of $10,760,905 in 2022. Enterprise Fund showed recovered expenses of $(484,435). Auditor is Reid Hurst Nagy Inc. based in Richmond BC. Salaries total estimated from segmented wages and benefits lines across Administration ($2,514,420), Education ($3,437,978), Health ($1,488,535), Social Assistance ($495,232), Treaty Lands and Resources ($3,652,659), Public Works ($1,696,225) = $13,285,049. Long-term debt includes BC Housing loan $406,551 and CMHC mortgages $3,541,664. Total government transfers estimated as ISC $6,502,442 + CMHC $197,139 + Government of Canada $2,134,011 + First Nations Health Authority $1,621,285 + Province of BC $3,548,196 + DFO $245,098 + FESC $760,117 + New Relationship Trust $0 = $15,008,288; adding BC Hydro $862,465 and BCFN Gaming $551,187 brings closer to $16,421,940; MST Employment and Training $646,195 added = ~$16,856,154 used as total government transfers estimate.
